response.justification: While Maria demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and infrastructure experience, she falls significantly short on two critical minimum qualifications: the role requires 8 years of C++ programming, but her resume shows approximately 6-12 months of professional C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014-2015) plus thesis work; similarly, the requirement for 5 years building large-scale cloud compute infrastructure and distributed systems is not met—her infrastructure work spans different contexts (pricing platforms, GIS applications) rather than cloud compute specialization. The technical leadership experience is present but not in cloud/compute-specific domains.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship foundation with proven expertise in TDD, CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker)
Demonstrated technical mentoring ability through organizing refactoring dojos, code katas, and creating training programs (Selenium course, Web Internals workshop)
Proven ability to own complex, ambiguous technical projects from inception to production (GISCollective, multiple full-stack platforms)
concerns: Critical gap in C++ depth: resume shows only ~6-12 months of professional C++ experience versus the required 8 years, with only brief mention at National Instruments and a thesis project
Limited specialization in large-scale cloud compute platforms or distributed compute systems—infrastructure experience is present but in different domains (pricing, GIS, web services)
Unclear evidence of leading cloud/compute engineering teams; staff titles present but scope of technical leadership over infrastructure/compute specialists is not well documented
